data.body:
Surfaced by FRAC-19's gap analysis + decisions doc (decision #6).

Why: Canonical foreground charcoal is #171717 (= --foreground HSL 0 0% 9%).
4 raw literal sites use #1a1a1a (drift, no visible difference). DESIGN.md's
"Don't use raw #1a1a1a" rule documents the canonical; this task normalizes.

Sites to fix:
- src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx:584
- src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx:801
- src/components/house/HouseBanner.tsx:96
- src/components/house/HouseBanner.tsx:97

Approach: replace each #1a1a1a string with the appropriate Tailwind
token reference (likely `text-foreground` or hex `#171717` depending
on context — JSX inline-style vs. Tailwind class). Visual diff:
imperceptible (~1% lightness change).

Scope: trivial (~4 edits, 2 files). ~15 min.

VERDICT: PASS

Reviewed cold against plan task_01KTD9RHVS0VH734N56ESS1JFT.md.

Diff verification (commit 2561940 only — the rest of master...HEAD is unrelated upstream merges of FRAC-19/23/41/42/43):
- Touches exactly 2 files, exactly 4 lines: src/components/house/HouseBanner.tsx (lines 96,97) and src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx (lines 584,801).
- Every change is literally "#1a1a1a" -> "hsl(var(--foreground))". No other edits, no whitespace churn, no type changes.
- Surrounding code (isDark branches, tooltipStyle signature, JSX consumers, function bodies) unchanged.

Acceptance criteria:
1. grep -ri "#1a1a1a" src/ -> zero hits. PASS.
2. pnpm build -> success, no warnings/errors related to color. PASS.
3. pnpm test -> 143 passed / 4 failed. The 4 failures are exactly the known baseline (footer Jacquard branding x2, navbar PC abbreviation, neighborhood min-h-screen flex). All unrelated to color logic. PASS.
4. Diff scope: exactly 2 files, exactly 4 lines. PASS.
5. isDark() classification unchanged. Stronger observation: the new "hsl(var(--foreground))" strings are output values only — they never feed back into isDark(), which is called on house.color / bgColor (true hex inputs). So the dark/light branching is structurally identical. PASS.

DESIGN.md cross-check:
- Decision context confirmed at lines 184, 316, 349: "raw #1a1a1a is charcoal drift; canonical charcoal is colors.foreground = #171717."
- src/index.css:47 declares --foreground: 0 0% 9%; /* #171717 - Deep charcoal/black */ — replacement materializes the correct token.

PRD (FRAC-22) cross-check: no impact on product surface, wayfinding, houses, or navigation. Pure conformance work, exactly as scoped.

Typecheck: npx tsc --noEmit -> clean (zero errors).

Minor observation (out of scope, not a fix request): DESIGN.md:184 still says "Four sites in the codebase still use #1a1a1a as a charcoal literal (OctahedronHero.tsx:584,801, HouseBanner.tsx:96-97)." After this PR that statement is stale — the four sites are no longer drift, they are normalized. Worth a follow-up doc update (separate task) but explicitly out-of-scope here (plan: "out of scope: any other normalization"). Did NOT touch DESIGN.md per review-phase conventions.

No inline fixes needed. No rework needed. Moving to done.
